title: Leaderboard
description: The leaderboard is the public view of system performance from completed evaluations. It helps you see which models perform best on which datasets and domains.

items: A public page showing ranked results from human evaluations (and, when available, automatic metrics).
Columns: overall rank (competition ranking), model name, average rate (μ), rate standard deviation (σ), dataset name, number of evaluations (votes), and metadata (e.g. organization, license).
You can pin models to keep them visible for comparison when scrolling.

subsections.title: How leaderboard scores are calculated and ranked

items: Model names may be anonymized in tasks (e.g. Model A, Model B); a mapping (task_models_shuffles) stores the real names for scoring.
For each model: aggregate rate and rank across all evaluated tasks; compute average rank (lower is better) and average rate (higher is better).
Sort: first by preference for better ranks (e.g. more 1st-place ranks), then by average rate to break ties.
Overall rank uses competition ranking: ties get the same rank; the next rank skips (e.g. 1, 2, 2, 4).

blocks.text: When available, a second tab shows automatic metrics (e.g. BLEU, chrF) per model and dataset. This allows comparison of human scores with reference-based metrics.
